---
title: "variable regressions"
author: "Jessica Larrick"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r 'post'(mandate period) indicator}
library(dplyr)
merged_cleanoutliers <- merged_cleanoutliers %>%
  mutate(
    PostMandate = ifelse(
      year_month >= "2015-04" & year_month <= "2017-04",
      1, 0
    )
  )
```

```{r stage one regression}
library(fixest)

stage1_model <- feols(
  pct_reduction ~ PostMandate:i(Stage_Num) + Total.Population.Served |
    WATER_SYSTEM_ID + year_month,
  data = merged_cleanoutliers,
  cluster = ~WATER_SYSTEM_ID
)
summary(stage1_model)

```

```{r setting up stage two}
library(dplyr)

# 1. Add fitted values from Stage 1
merged_cleanoutliers <- merged_cleanoutliers %>%
  mutate(fitted = predict(stage1_model, newdata = merged_cleanoutliers))

# 2. Compute supplier-level effectiveness over the emergency window
eff_2015 <- merged_cleanoutliers %>%
  filter(PostMandate == 1) %>%   # Apr 2015 – Apr 2017
  group_by(WATER_SYSTEM_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    eff2015 = mean(fitted, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 3. Compute strictness in 2015 (max stage reached in 2015)
stage2015 <- merged_cleanoutliers %>%
  filter(Year == 2015) %>%
  group_by(WATER_SYSTEM_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    Stage2015 = max(Stage_Num, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 4. Merge both into the dataset
merged_cleanoutliers <- merged_cleanoutliers %>%
  left_join(eff_2015,  by = "WATER_SYSTEM_ID") %>%
  left_join(stage2015, by = "WATER_SYSTEM_ID")
```

```{r building a supplier-level dataset for stage two (rather than supplier-year-month)}
stage2_data <- merged_cleanoutliers %>%
  group_by(WATER_SYSTEM_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    # All tactic variables (Used_*_2020, Used_*_2021, etc.)
    across(matches("^Used_"), ~ first(.x)),

    # Time-invariant institutional characteristics
    IsPublic = first(IsPublic),
    Hydrologic.Region = first(Hydrologic.Region),

    # 2015 drought-year predictors
    eff2015 = first(eff2015),
    Stage2015 = first(Stage2015),

    .groups = "drop"
  )

```

```{r making a stable(invariant and reproducible) measure for population by year}
# Pop 2020
pop2020 <- merged_cleanoutliers %>%
  filter(Year == 2020) %>%
  arrange(year_month) %>%
  group_by(WATER_SYSTEM_ID) %>%
  summarise(Pop2020 = first(Total.Population.Served), .groups = "drop")

# Pop 2021
pop2021 <- merged_cleanoutliers %>%
  filter(Year == 2021) %>%
  arrange(year_month) %>%
  group_by(WATER_SYSTEM_ID) %>%
  summarise(Pop2021 = first(Total.Population.Served), .groups = "drop")

# Pop 2022
pop2022 <- merged_cleanoutliers %>%
  filter(Year == 2022) %>%
  arrange(year_month) %>%
  group_by(WATER_SYSTEM_ID) %>%
  summarise(Pop2022 = first(Total.Population.Served), .groups = "drop")

# Pop 2023
pop2023 <- merged_cleanoutliers %>%
  filter(Year == 2023) %>%
  arrange(year_month) %>%
  group_by(WATER_SYSTEM_ID) %>%
  summarise(Pop2023 = first(Total.Population.Served), .groups = "drop")

#merge all population years into Stage 2
stage2_data <- stage2_data %>%
  left_join(pop2020, by = "WATER_SYSTEM_ID") %>%
  left_join(pop2021, by = "WATER_SYSTEM_ID") %>%
  left_join(pop2022, by = "WATER_SYSTEM_ID") %>%
  left_join(pop2023, by = "WATER_SYSTEM_ID")
```

```{r stage two regression, code to run all years at once for a given tactic}
years <- 2020:2023
models <- list()

for (y in years) {
  
  #use correct population variable name
  pop_var <- paste0("Pop", y)
  
  #build the formula dynamically
  formula <- as.formula(
    paste0(
      "Used_Rebate_", y,
      " ~ eff2015 * Stage2015 + ",
      pop_var,
      " + IsPublic + Hydrologic.Region"
    )
  )
  
  #run the model
  models[[as.character(y)]] <- feols(
    formula,
    data = stage2_data,
    cluster = ~ WATER_SYSTEM_ID
  )
}

#results
lapply(models, summary)

library(modelsummary)

tab <- modelsummary(
  models,
  output = "data.frame",
  estimate  = "{estimate}",
  statistic = "({std.error})",
  stars = c("*" = .1, "**" = .05, "***" = .01),
  gof_omit = "IC|Log|Adj|Pseudo"
)

```

```{r code for output tables}
library(modelsummary)
library(flextable)
library(tibble)

#specify which coefficients to include and labels
coef_map <- c(
  "eff2015" = "Effective 2015",
  "Stage2015" = "Stage 2015",
  "eff2015:Stage2015" = "Effective × Stage",
  "IsPublic" = "Public Utility"
)

#goodness-of-fit rows and labels
gof_map <- tribble(
  ~raw,          ~clean,          ~fmt,
  "nobs",        "Observations",   0,
  "r.squared",   "R-squared",      3,
  "rmse",        "RMSE",           3
)

#build table
ft <- modelsummary(
  models,
  output = "flextable",
  coef_map = coef_map,
  gof_map = gof_map,
  estimate  = "{estimate}{stars}",
  statistic = "({std.error})",
  stars = c("*" = .1, "**" = .05, "***" = .01)
)

#format table
ft <- ft |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  autofit() |>
  bold(part = "header") |>
  align(align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_footer_lines(values = c(
    "* p < 0.1, ** p < 0.05, *** p < 0.01"
  ))

#export
save_as_docx(ft, path = "RebateResults.docx")

```
